L1.area: Model artifact retrieval and resource management utilities

L2.description: An absolute path traversal vulnerability exists where archived artifacts with entries containing absolute path names are not properly sanitized during extraction. A crafted archive can include entries with leading file separator characters, causing files to be written to arbitrary locations on the filesystem instead of the intended target directory, potentially overwriting critical system files.

L3.area: Model artifact retrieval and resource management utilities
L3.description: An absolute path traversal vulnerability exists where archived artifacts with entries containing absolute path names are not properly sanitized during extraction. A crafted archive can include entries with leading file separator characters, causing files to be written to arbitrary locations on the filesystem instead of the intended target directory, potentially overwriting critical system files.

ground_truth.reason: The archive extraction utilities do not strip leading file separator characters from entry names, allowing archives with absolute paths to write files to arbitrary system locations.
